title: Experimental_investigation_of_thermoacoustic_coupling_using
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 4: Mean OH concentration (right) and Rayleigh Index (left) at 125 Hz acoustic forcing. (P = 1 atm)**

    *   **Axes:**

        *   Width (cm) on the horizontal axis, with values ranging from 0 to 9
        *   Vertical axis (left of each plot represents Ryleigh Index with range varying based on fuel mixture)
        *   Vertical axis (right of each plot represents OH concentration with range varying based on fuel mixture)
    *   **Curves:**

        *   Contour plots illustrating mean OH concentration and Rayleigh index distribution in the combustion zone. The shapes vary depending on fuel mixture (7%H2-93%CH4, 20%H2-80%CH4, 30%H2-70%CH4)

    *   **Key values:** Acoustic forcing at 125Hz, P = 1 atm
        * 7%H2-93%CH4: Rayleigh Index range spans from -0.8 to 0.8. OH concentration range spans from 0.2 x 10^4 to 1.8 x 10^4.
        * 20%H2-80%CH4: Rayleigh Index range spans from -1 to 1. OH concentration range spans from 1 x 10^4 to 6 x 10^4.
        * 30%H2-70%CH4: Rayleigh Index range spans from -0.6 to 0.6. OH concentration range spans from 0.5 x 10^4 to 2 x 10^4.

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Mentions "Fig. 1. A photo of the experiment system and an experiment schematic are shown in Fig 1.", but no explicit description of components or labels are provided other than: Laser system and its associated components, the laser transmission and sheet forming optics, the burner section (burner, pre-mixer, swirler, and gas supply system)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   **Fuel mixtures:** 7%H2-93%CH4, 20%H2-80%CH4, 30%H2-70%CH4.
*   **Swirl number:** 0.5
*   **Vane angle:** 65 degrees
*   **Acoustic forcing frequency range:** 60 Hz to 180 Hz
*   **Acoustic forcing amplitude:** 0.03% to 0.5% of mean pressure
*   **Chamber pressure:** at least 3 bars for vortex-tripping mechanism. Commercial gas turbines: 20-30 bar
*   **Pressure oscillations frequency:** 399 Hz. Pressure variation less than 7%
*   **Forcing Frequency:** 125 Hz in figure 4
*   **Diameter of acoustic speakers:** 30 cm
*   **Power amplifier:** 2600 W (Mackie M1400i)

    *   Color bar axes: x 10^4

**3. Tables:**
    *Table 1 (mentioned in text as source of experimental conditions but not visible in picture)*

**4. Diagrams:**

*   No diagrams

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Acoustic amplitude: 0.05% of mean chamber pressure
*   (CH/CA)st = 0.418 (stoichiometric hydrogen to air molar ratio)
*   Forcing Frequencies: 85, 125, 222, and 399 Hz
*   Pressures: Mean OH concentration (right) and Rayleigh Index (left) at 222 Hz acoustic forcing.  (P = 1 atm)

*   Rayleigh Index uncertainty: approximately 10% of local intensity (for strong signal regions)
*   Flame intermittence excursions: 50% to 100%
*   Relative error in OH PLIF images: typically 5% or less
*   Flame length reduction (7% hydrogen): ~20%
*   Flame length reduction (other cases): approximately 30%
*   Flame length (30% hydrogen): ~5 cm
